chunks.text:
# Manufacturing Business Glossary

This glossary defines the core business concepts and terminology used in the manufacturing supply chain domain. It covers product structures, materials management, production workflows, and quality assurance processes.

## Product

A Product represents any manufactured item in the system, including finished goods ready for sale, sub-assemblies used in production, and individual components. Products are organized in a hierarchical structure where complex product... [TRUNCATED]

## Warehouse

A Warehouse represents a physical storage location where materials, components, and finished goods are stored. Warehouses have defined capacity limits, geographic location information, and are managed by designated managers. Warehouses serve as nodes in the supply chain network where inventory is held, shipments are received, and products are staged for production or distribution.

**Key Attributes:**
- Unique warehouse identifier
- Warehouse name and location
- Address, city, and ... [TRUNCATED]

## QualityControl

Quality Control (QC) represents inspection and testing activities performed on materials, in-process production, or finished goods. QC records track the type of inspection (incoming, in-process, final), inspection date, inspector, test results (pass, fail, conditional), defect counts, and notes. QC activities are associated with specific production batches and may reference technical specifications being tested.

**Key Attributes:**
- Unique QC identifier
- Batch reference (pr... [TRUNCATED]

# Manufacturing Data Dictionary

This data dictionary documents the database schema for the manufacturing supply chain system. It includes all tables, columns, data types, constraints, and relationships. Special attention is given to self-referential relationships that support hierarchical product structures.

## product

Stores all manufactured items including finished goods, assemblies, and components. Supports hierarchical product relationships via self-referencing foreign key.

| Column | Da... [TRUNCATED]

## bom

Bill of Materials defining product composition and hierarchical structure. Supports recursive relationships for multi-level assemblies.

| Column | Data Type | Constraints | Description |
|--------|-----------|-------------|-------------|
| bom_id | VARCHAR(20) | PRIMARY KEY | Unique BOM identifier |
| parent_product_id | VARCHAR(20) | NOT NULL, FOREIGN KEY → product(product_id) | Parent product (what is being built) |
| component_product_id | VARCHAR(20) | NOT NULL, FOREIGN KEY → produc... [TRUNCATED]

## warehouse

Stores physical storage location information for inventory management.

| Column | Data Type | Constraints | Description |
|--------|-----------|-------------|-------------|
| warehouse_id | VARCHAR(20) | PRIMARY KEY | Unique warehouse identifier |
| warehouse_name | VARCHAR(100) | NOT NULL | Warehouse name |
| address | VARCHAR(200) | | Street address |
| city | VARCHAR(50) | | City location |
| state | VARCHAR(50) | | State or province |
| capacity | INT | NOT NULL, DEFAULT 0, CH... [TRUNCATED]

## work_order

Represents production jobs for manufacturing products with hierarchical work order support.

| Column | Data Type | Constraints | Description |
|--------|-----------|-------------|-------------|
| work_order_id | VARCHAR(20) | PRIMARY KEY | Unique work order identifier |
| product_id | VARCHAR(20) | NOT NULL, FOREIGN KEY → product(product_id) | Product to manufacture |
| parent_work_order | VARCHAR(20) | FOREIGN KEY → work_order(work_order_id) | Parent work order for decomposition... [TRUNCATED]

## specification

Defines technical requirements and acceptance criteria for materials and products.

| Column | Data Type | Constraints | Description |
|--------|-----------|-------------|-------------|
| specification_id | VARCHAR(20) | PRIMARY KEY | Unique specification identifier |
| specification_name | VARCHAR(100) | NOT NULL | Specification name |
| version | VARCHAR(10) | NOT NULL | Specification version |
| effective_date | DATE | NOT NULL | Effective date |
| spec_type | spec_type_t | ... [TRUNCATED]

## shipment

Tracks material transfers between warehouses, suppliers, and customers.

| Column | Data Type | Constraints | Description |
|--------|-----------|-------------|-------------|
| shipment_id | VARCHAR(20) | PRIMARY KEY | Unique shipment identifier |
| shipment_type | shipment_type_t | NOT NULL | INBOUND, OUTBOUND, INTERNAL |
| warehouse_id | VARCHAR(20) | FOREIGN KEY → warehouse(warehouse_id) | Warehouse reference |
| supplier_id | VARCHAR(20) | FOREIGN KEY → supplier(supplier_id) | S... [TRUNCATED]

entities_post_resolution.name: Pending
entities_post_resolution.definition: MEDIUM is a mid-level priority value within a task prioritization schema (LOW, MEDIUM, HIGH, URGENT) used to rank the urgency of work items.
entities_post_resolution.provenance_text: | status | status_t | NOT NULL, DEFAULT 'PENDING' | PENDING, IN_PROGRESS, COMPLETED, CLOSED | | | priority | priority_t | NOT NULL, DEFAULT 'MEDIUM' | LOW, MEDIUM, HIGH, URGENT |

entities_post_resolution.name: Product
entities_post_resolution.definition: Product is the manufacturing-domain entity representing any manufactured item in the system, including finished goods, assemblies, and components, with attributes such as name, type, cost, lead time, and hierarchical relationships via BOM.
entities_post_resolution.provenance_text:
# Product

A Product represents any manufactured item in the system, including finished goods ready for sale, sub-assemblies used in production, and individual components. | Each product has a unique identifier, name, type classification, base cost, and lead time for procurement or production. | Products can be active or inactive based on current manufacturing requirements. | A Product can have multiple child Products (via BOM hierarchy) | A Product can reference a parent Product (for multi-level assemblies) | Products are used in Work Orders for production | Products appear in Shipments (outbound) | Products are stored in Inventory records | Work Orders produce Products | - A product can contain other products as components via BOM | BOM connects Products to other Products (parent-child) | product

Stores all manufactured items including finished goods, assemblies, and components. | product

Stores all manufactured items including finished goods, assemblies, and components. Supports h

entities_post_resolution.name: Production Batch
entities_post_resolution.definition: A production batch (also called a lot) is a group of units produced in a single manufacturing run, tracked for traceability, inventory management, expiry if applicable, and quality control.
entities_post_resolution.provenance_text: A Batch represents a specific production lot or run of a product, tracking all units produced together in a single manufacturing cycle. | Batches enable traceability for quality issues and inventory management for time-sensitive or lot-controlled products. | - QC inspections are performed on Batches | Batches record the product produced, production date, quantity produced, warehouse location, expiry date (if applicable), and quality control status.

entities_post_resolution.name: Component
entities_post_resolution.definition: A Component is a basic part or raw material used in a product's bill of materials, identified by a unique component_id, potentially linked to approved suppliers via a component_supplier relationship, and referenced in BOM entries as the component (which may be either a component or a sub-assembly).
entities_post_resolution.provenance_text:
A Component represents atomic raw materials or purchased parts that cannot be further broken down into simpler manufactured items. | Components are supplied by multiple Suppliers (many-to-many) | Components have Inventory records tracking stock levels | Components may reference Specifications for quality requirements | Suppliers provide multiple Components (many-to-many via component_supplier) | component

Stores atomic raw materials and purchased parts that are procured from external suppliers. | Relationships:
- Many-to-many with supplier via component_supplier junction table | Relationships:
- One-to-many with inventory | Relationships:
- Optional relationship with specification | Each BOM entry indicates a parent product, a component product (which can be either a component or sub-assembly), the required quantity, unit of measure, and hierarchical level in the product structure. | | component_id | VARCHAR(20) | NOT NULL, FOREIGN KEY → component(component_id) | Component reference |

entities_post_resolution.name: Inventory Record
entities_post_resolution.definition: An inventory record is the data unit that records stock levels (on hand and reserved) and related attributes for a specific item at a specific location, identified by a unique inventory_id.
entities_post_resolution.provenance_text:
Each inventory record tracks the quantity on hand, quantity reserved for pending orders, and reorder thresholds to trigger procurement. | Inventory records support either components or products (but not both in the same record) and include timestamps for the last restock date. | Inventory belongs to a Warehouse | Inventory tracks either a Component or Product | Work Orders consume Inventory (components) | Products are stored in Inventory records | Components have Inventory records tracking stock levels | Warehouses serve as nodes in the supply chain network where inventory is held, shipments are received, and products are staged for production or distribution. | ## inventory

Tracks stock levels for components and products at specific warehouse locations. | | inventory_id | VARCHAR(20) | PRIMARY KEY | Unique inventory identifier |

entities_post_resolution.name: Quantity Reserved
entities_post_resolution.definition: The quantity of inventory that has been reserved or allocated for pending orders and is not available for immediate fulfillment.
entities_post_resolution.provenance_text: | quantity_reserved | DECIMAL(10,2) | NOT NULL, DEFAULT 0.00, CHECK >= 0 | Reserved quantity | | Each inventory record tracks the quantity on hand, quantity reserved for pending orders, and reorder thresholds to trigger procurement.

entities_post_resolution.name: Shipment
entities_post_resolution.definition: Shipment is the entity representing the physical transfer of materials between supply chain locations (e.g., suppliers, warehouses, customers), including associated attributes such as shipment type (INBOUND, OUTBOUND, INTERNAL) and shipment status (PENDING, SHIPPED, DELIVERED, CANCELLED).
entities_post_resolution.provenance_text: A Shipment represents the physical transfer of materials between warehouses, from suppliers to warehouses (inbound), or from warehouses to customers (outbound). | Shipments track shipment type, source and destination locations, dates for ship, estimated arrival, and actual delivery, along with current status. | Shipments update Inventory levels | Shipments fulfill Work Order outputs | Products appear in Shipments (outbound) | Warehouses serve as nodes in the supply chain network where inventory is held, shipments are received, and products are staged for production or distribution. | Tracks material transfers between warehouses, suppliers, and customers. | | status | shipment_status_t | NOT NULL, DEFAULT 'PENDING' | PENDING, SHIPPED, DELIVERED, CANCELLED | | | shipment_type | shipment_type_t | NOT NULL | INBOUND, OUTBOUND, INTERNAL |

entities_post_resolution.name: Specification
entities_post_resolution.definition: A specification is a formal document that defines technical requirements, standards, and acceptance criteria for materials or products, used to determine compliance and guide quality and testing.
entities_post_resolution.provenance_text: A Specification defines technical requirements, standards, and acceptance criteria for materials or products. | Components may reference Specifications for quality requirements | - QC tests against Specifications | Specifications have version control for engineering changes

entities_post_resolution.name: Capacity
entities_post_resolution.definition: The storage capacity attribute (column capacity) that defines the maximum storage space available, represented as a positive integer in the data model.
entities_post_resolution.provenance_text: | capacity | INT | NOT NULL, DEFAULT 0, CHECK > 0 | Storage capacity |

entities_post_resolution.name: Street Address
entities_post_resolution.definition: The street address of a location, stored as a text field in an address/contact dataset.
entities_post_resolution.provenance_text: | address | VARCHAR(200) | | Street address |

entities_post_resolution.name: Unique Inventory Identifier
entities_post_resolution.definition: A unique identifier used to distinguish and retrieve a specific inventory record, typically implemented as the primary key (inventory_id) for the inventory domain.
entities_post_resolution.provenance_text: | inventory_id | VARCHAR(20) | PRIMARY KEY | Unique inventory identifier | | | warehouse_id | VARCHAR(20) | PRIMARY KEY | Unique warehouse identifier | | Each product has a unique identifier, name, type classification, base cost, and lead time for procurement or production.

entities_post_resolution.name: Warehouse
entities_post_resolution.definition: A Warehouse is a physical storage location for inventory with a unique identifier and a name, used to store materials and finished goods in inventory management.
entities_post_resolution.provenance_text:
A Warehouse represents a physical storage location where materials, components, and finished goods are stored. | Warehouses have defined capacity limits, geographic location information, and are managed by designated managers. | Warehouses serve as nodes in the supply chain network where inventory is held, shipments are received, and products are staged for production or distribution. | Inventory belongs to a Warehouse | | warehouse_name | VARCHAR(100) | NOT NULL | Warehouse name | | Work Orders are associated with Warehouses | ## warehouse

Stores physical storage location information for inventory management. | | warehouse_id | VARCHAR(20) | NOT NULL, FOREIGN KEY → warehouse(warehouse_id) | Warehouse location | | | warehouse_id | VARCHAR(20) | FOREIGN KEY → warehouse(warehouse_id) | Production location | | | warehouse_id | VARCHAR(20) | FOREIGN KEY → warehouse(warehouse_id) | Warehouse reference | | | warehouse_id | VARCHAR(20) | PRIMARY KEY | Unique warehouse identifier |

entities_post_resolution.name: Work Order
entities_post_resolution.definition: A work_order is a production job/instruction in manufacturing used to plan, schedule, execute, and track the production of a specified quantity of a product, potentially with hierarchical relationships and a unique identifier.
entities_post_resolution.provenance_text:
## work_order

Represents production jobs for manufacturing products with hierarchical work order support. | | parent_work_order | VARCHAR(20) | FOREIGN KEY → work_order(work_order_id) | Parent work order for decomposition | | Products are used in Work Orders for production | A Work Order represents a production job or manufacturing run to produce a specified quantity of a product. | Work Orders track planned versus completed quantities, status through the production lifecycle, priority levels, and planned dates. | Work Orders can have hierarchical relationships where a parent work order may decompose into child work orders for different production stages or sub-assemblies. | Work Orders produce Products | Work Orders consume Inventory (components) | Work Orders are associated with Warehouses | Routes are used for scheduling Work Orders | | work_order_id | VARCHAR(20) | PRIMARY KEY | Unique work order identifier |

entities_post_resolution.name: Base Cost
entities_post_resolution.definition: The baseline manufacturing cost of a product used for cost calculations in production or procurement.
entities_post_resolution.provenance_text: | base_cost | DECIMAL(10,2) | NOT NULL, DEFAULT 0.00, CHECK >= 0 | Base manufacturing cost | | Each product has a unique identifier, name, type classification, base cost, and lead time for procurement or production.

entities_post_resolution.name: Product Composition
entities_post_resolution.definition: Product composition is the set of components and sub-assemblies and their hierarchical relationships defined by the Bill of Materials to manufacture a parent product.
entities_post_resolution.provenance_text: Bill of Materials defining product composition and hierarchical structure. | The Bill of Materials (BOM) defines the composition of products by specifying which components and sub-assemblies are required to manufacture a parent product.

entities_post_resolution.name: Ship Date
entities_post_resolution.definition: The ship_date is the date on which a shipment is dispatched from the origin and used to track and schedule logistics.
entities_post_resolution.provenance_text: | ship_date | DATE | NOT NULL | Shipment date | | Shipments track shipment type, source and destination locations, dates for ship, estimated arrival, and actual delivery, along with current status.

entities_post_resolution.name: Parent Product Id
entities_post_resolution.definition: The parent product in a multi-level bill of materials/product assembly, represented by a foreign key (parent_product_id) that references the product's unique identifier (product_id).
entities_post_resolution.provenance_text:
| parent_product_id | VARCHAR(20) | FOREIGN KEY → product(product_id) | Parent product for hierarchical assemblies | | Self-Referential Relationship:
- `parent_product_id` references `product_id` — enables multi-level product assemblies | | parent_product_id | VARCHAR(20) | NOT NULL, FOREIGN KEY → product(product_id) | Parent product (what is being built) | | Indexes: - `idx_bom_parent` on parent_product_id — BOM explosion queries | Self-Referential Structure: - Both `parent_product_id` and `component_product_id` reference the product table | A Product can reference a parent Product (for multi-level assemblies) | Each BOM entry indicates a parent product, a component product (which can be either a component or sub-assembly), the required quantity, unit of measure, and hierarchical level in the product structure. | | component_product_id | VARCHAR(20) | NOT NULL, FOREIGN KEY → product(product_id) | Component product (what goes into it) | | | product_id | VARCHAR(20) | FOREIGN KEY → prod

entities_post_resolution.name: Planned Start Date
entities_post_resolution.definition: The planned end date is the date by which a task, milestone, or project is expected to be completed according to the project plan.
entities_post_resolution.provenance_text: | planned_start_date | DATE | | Planned start date | | | planned_end_date | DATE | | Planned end date |

entities_post_resolution.name: Qc Type
entities_post_resolution.definition: Represents the category or kind of quality-control checks applied to data items, with lifecycle stages such as INCOMING, IN_PROCESS, and FINAL.
entities_post_resolution.provenance_text: | qc_type | qc_type_t | NOT NULL | INCOMING, IN_PROCESS, FINAL |

entities_post_resolution.name: Quantity On Hand
entities_post_resolution.definition: The on-hand quantity represents the amount of inventory currently available in stock (not reserved) that can be used to fulfill orders.
entities_post_resolution.provenance_text: | quantity_on_hand | DECIMAL(10,2) | NOT NULL, DEFAULT 0.00, CHECK >= 0 | Available quantity | | Each inventory record tracks the quantity on hand, quantity reserved for pending orders, and reorder thresholds to trigger procurement.

entities_post_resolution.name: Supplier(Supplier Id)
entities_post_resolution.definition: The supplier_id foreign key referencing the supplier table's supplier_id, used to identify the supplier associated with a record (inbound supplier reference).
entities_post_resolution.provenance_text: | supplier_id | VARCHAR(20) | NOT NULL, FOREIGN KEY → supplier(supplier_id) | Supplier reference | | | supplier_id | VARCHAR(20) | FOREIGN KEY → supplier(supplier_id) | Supplier (inbound only) |

entities_post_resolution.name: Unit Of Measure
entities_post_resolution.definition: Unit of measure is the quantity unit (e.g., EA, KG, M, L) used to express the amount of a component within a BOM and in related data fields.
entities_post_resolution.provenance_text: | unit_of_measure | VARCHAR(10) | NOT NULL, DEFAULT 'EA' | Unit of measure (EA, KG, M, L, etc.) | | | unit_of_measure | VARCHAR(10) | NOT NULL, DEFAULT 'EA' | Unit of measure | | Each BOM entry indicates a parent product, a component product (which can be either a component or sub-assembly), the required quantity, unit of measure, and hierarchical level in the product structure.

entities_post_resolution.name: Last Restock Date
entities_post_resolution.definition: The date of the most recent restock event for an inventory item, stored as last_restock_date in inventory records.
entities_post_resolution.provenance_text: | last_restock_date | DATE | | Last restock timestamp | | Inventory records support either components or products (but not both in the same record) and include timestamps for the last restock date.

entities_post_resolution.name: Null Default
entities_post_resolution.definition: Represents the required quantity to be produced, stored as a positive decimal with a default of 1.000.
entities_post_resolution.provenance_text: | base_cost | DECIMAL(10,2) | NOT NULL, DEFAULT 0.00, CHECK >= 0 | Base manufacturing cost | | | standard_cost | DECIMAL(10,2) | NOT NULL, DEFAULT 0.00, CHECK >= 0 | Standard procurement cost | | | unit_price | DECIMAL(10,2) | NOT NULL, DEFAULT 0.00, CHECK >= 0 | Negotiated unit price | | | product_name | VARCHAR(100) | NOT NULL | Product name | | | component_name | VARCHAR(100) | NOT NULL | Component name | | | unit_of_measure | VARCHAR(10) | NOT NULL, DEFAULT 'EA' | Unit of measure (EA, KG, M, L, etc.) | | | product_type | product_type_t | NOT NULL, DEFAULT 'FINISHED_GOOD' | Product classification: FINISHED_GOOD, ASSEMBLY, COMPONENT | | | is_optional | yes_no_t | NOT NULL, DEFAULT 'N' | Whether component is optional | | | is_active | yes_no_t | NOT NULL, DEFAULT 'Y' | Active status flag | | | lead_time_days | INT | NOT NULL, DEFAULT 0 | Procurement lead time | | | lead_time_days | INT | NOT NULL, DEFAULT 0, CHECK >= 0 | Procurement or production lead time | | | bom_level | INT | NOT

entities_post_resolution.name: Reorder Threshold
entities_post_resolution.definition: The quantity threshold at which an item should be reordered to replenish stock.
entities_post_resolution.provenance_text: | reorder_threshold | DECIMAL(10,2) | NOT NULL, DEFAULT 0.00 | Reorder trigger level | | Each inventory record tracks the quantity on hand, quantity reserved for pending orders, and reorder thresholds to trigger procurement.

entities_post_resolution.name: Route
entities_post_resolution.definition: A Route is the defined sequence of manufacturing operations or steps that a product must pass through, used to schedule work orders and determine capacity.
entities_post_resolution.provenance_text: A Route defines the sequence of manufacturing operations or steps required to produce a product. | Route steps are ordered by sequence_number | Routes determine production capacity requirements | Routes are used for scheduling Work Orders

entities_post_resolution.name: Recursive Relationships
entities_post_resolution.definition: An entity relationship where records relate to other records of the same type to form a hierarchical, multi-level structure (parent-child) used to model assemblies or decompositions such as bills of materials and work orders.
entities_post_resolution.provenance_text:
Self-Referential Relationship:
- `parent_product_id` references `product_id` — enables multi-level product assemblies | Work Orders can have hierarchical relationships where a parent work order may decompose into child work orders for different production stages or sub-assemblies. | Bill of Materials defining product composition and hierarchical structure. Supports recursive relationships for multi-level assemblies.

entities_post_resolution.name: Varchar(20)
entities_post_resolution.definition: VARCHAR(20) represents a variable-length character string data type with a maximum length of 20 characters, used for the specification_id field in technical specification references.
entities_post_resolution.provenance_text: | specification_id | VARCHAR(20) | | Technical specification reference | | | component_category | VARCHAR(50) | | Component category classification |

entities_post_resolution.name: Lead Time Days
entities_post_resolution.definition: Lead time in days is the duration from ordering to delivery for procurement or production of a component or product.
entities_post_resolution.provenance_text: | lead_time_days | INT | NOT NULL, DEFAULT 0, CHECK >= 0 | Procurement or production lead time | | | lead_time_days | INT | NOT NULL, DEFAULT 0 | Procurement lead time | | Each product has a unique identifier, name, type classification, base cost, and lead time for procurement or production. | Suppliers have negotiated pricing and lead times per component

entities_post_resolution.provenance_text: | quantity_on_hand | DECIMAL(10,2) | NOT NULL, DEFAULT 0.00, CHECK >= 0 | Available quantity |

tables_enriched.name: Product
tables_enriched.description: Stores information about products, including identifiers, names, types, hierarchical relationships to parent products, base cost, lead time in days, and whether the product is currently active.

tables_enriched.name: Component
tables_enriched.description: Stores information about individual components, including identifiers, names, categories, measurement units, standard cost, and associated specification references.

tables_enriched.name: Bill of Materials
tables_enriched.description: Stores the bill of materials relationships, detailing which components (PRODUCT) are used to compose a parent product, along with quantities and where the item sits in the BOM. It also indicates wheth... [TRUNCATED]

tables_enriched.name: Supplier
tables_enriched.description: Stores information about suppliers, including contact details, rating, and whether they are a preferred supplier.

tables_enriched.name: WAREHOUSE
tables_enriched.description: Stores information about each warehouse, including identification, location details, capacity, and the manager responsible for the facility.

tables_enriched.name: Inventory
tables_enriched.description: Stores current inventory levels and restocking information for products across warehouses, including quantities on hand, quantity reserved, reorder threshold, and the date of the last restock.

tables_enriched.name: Work Order
tables_enriched.description: Stores data about work orders, including product, quantities, status and timing, with references to potential parent work orders and the warehouse responsible for fulfillment.

tables_enriched.name: Shipment
tables_enriched.description: Stores data about shipments, including identifiers, involved warehouse and supplier, associated customer, key dates, and current status of each shipment.

tables_enriched.name: Quality Control
tables_enriched.description: Stores records of quality control checks performed on batches, including the type of check, the inspector, the date, results, and any defect counts or notes.

tables_enriched.name: Specification
tables_enriched.description: Stores specifications and their numeric bounds and units for validation and reference data used in products or processes.

tables_enriched.name: Batch
tables_enriched.description: Stores records of production batches, including what product was produced, quantities, production and expiry dates, the warehouse storing the batch, and the QC status.

tables_enriched.name: Route
tables_enriched.description: Stores the sequence of operations for manufacturing routes, detailing the route identifiers, associated product, operation steps, work centers, and the time required for setup and cycle of each step.

tables_enriched.name: Component Supplier
tables_enriched.description: Stores associations between components and suppliers, including whether a supplier is preferred, expected lead time in days, and the unit price for the component from that supplier.
